prompt: Perform a comprehensive configuration drift audit across a distributed system's configuration files. The config directory is at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/configs/'. There are 8 subdirectories representing different environments: production/, staging/, development/, testing/, qa/, pre-prod/, load-test/, and disaster-recovery/. Each environment directory contains multiple configuration files: app.json (application settings, ~200 lines), database.yml (database connections, ~150 lines), redis.conf (cache configuration, ~100 lines), nginx.conf (reverse proxy settings, ~200 lines), logging.json (log levels and outputs, ~100 lines), secrets.env (environment variables template, ~80 lines), and features.json (feature flags, ~150 lines). Your task: 1) Read every configuration file in every environment directory (that's 56 files total). 2) For each configuration key found across environments, compare the values and identify: keys that exist in some environments but not others (configuration drift), keys with different values across environments that probably should be the same (inconsistencies), keys with identical values across all environments that might indicate copy-paste without customization (potential misconfiguration). 3) Specifically check: all database hostnames and ports, all redis connection strings, all logging levels, all feature flag states, all nginx server_name and upstream directives. 4) Flag any environment that appears to have production credentials in its secrets.env template. 5) Generate a comprehensive drift report at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/reports/config-drift-audit.txt' with sections: Executive Summary, Drift Matrix (key x environment grid showing which environments diverge), Critical Findings (security issues, missing configs), Recommendations (which configs to synchronize, which environments need attention), and a full appendix listing every configuration key and its value in each environment. CRITICAL: This is for a healthcare provider under HIPAA audit. You must also: 1) Compare against CIS benchmarks. 2) Check for encryption at rest and in transit. 3) Validate access controls and audit logging. 4) Generate compliance scorecards. 5) Create remediation playbooks with timelines.
